question.content: Write one constitutional isomer of compound C that satisfies all listed constraints: it contains a phenolic hydroxyl group, gives a color reaction with ferric chloride solution, and has three proton NMR groups with integral ratio 9:6:2. Answer format: one condensed structural formula in ASCII; write only the formula, with no explanatory text, spaces, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Construct an aromatic phenol isomer with one tert-butyl substituent and two equivalent methyl substituents.
step_1.key_point: This arrangement provides proton-set integrals 9H, 6H, and 2H.

step_3.content: Check that two methyl groups are equivalent and two aromatic protons remain equivalent under ring symmetry.
step_3.key_point: A valid condensed structural expression can be output directly.
